chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss

text: 3932 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي نَصْرٍ عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ سِرْحَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "فِي رَجُلٍ حَمَلَ مَتَاعاً عَلَى رَأْسِهِ فَأَصَابَ إِنْسَاناً فَمَاتَ أَوْ اِنْكَسَرَ مِنْهُ شَيْءٌ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3932 - Ibn Abi Nasr narrated from Dawood bin Sirhan, from Abu Abdillah (as):
"If a man carries goods on his head and accidentally strikes someone, causing their death or breaking something, then he is liable."
